November 07, 2025

AI and the Economy

Vice Chair Philip N. Jefferson

At Euro20+, Deutsche Bundesbank, Frankfurt, Germany

Thank you, President Nagel and the Bundesbank, for the opportunity to meet with you today. I am excited to be here. I will make sure I leave plenty of time for questions. But I would like to start by speaking with you about a topic that's been on my mind: artificial intelligence (AI).

Often, when I gather with students, consumers, or businesspeople, the conversation quickly turns to AI. AI has the potential to transform how we work and how we live, and it could have a profound effect on the economy. Today, I will talk about AI's rapid growth and how I approach thinking about AI by examining it through the lens of the dual mandate given to the Federal Reserve by the U.S. Congress.

AI's Rapid Growth
In a few short years, AI morphed from the realm of science fiction to a tool that high schoolers use to help them do their science homework.
